answer:
In Allama Iqbal's poem *Aashiq Harjaai* ("The Roving Lover" or "The Faithless Lover"), the titular *harjaai* (faithless or capricious) lover offers a vivid, paradoxical self-portrait as a restless, contradictory figure—vibrant yet solitary, celestial yet earthly, devout yet irreverent, wise yet mad, ever-changing and disloyal in love, embodying the mercurial nature of quicksilver.[^1]

The full account unfolds across the poem's stanzas:

> The Roving Lover[^2]
> 
> What a strange collection of contradictions you are, Iqbal!  
> You are the life of the gathering, and yet you are also alone.[^3]
> 
> From your clamor, O wild, melodious soul,  
> You are the life of the garden, and the grace of the desert too.[^4]
> 
> Companion to the stars in your high flight,  
> Yet you tread the earth, your steps also measuring the sky.[^5]
> 
> In the midst of worldly pleasure, your brow is bowed in prayer;  
> Your path holds something of the creed of the wine-cup.[^6]
> 
> Like a flower’s scent, you are free from the garment of color;  
> You are a creator of wisdom, yet you possess a touch of madness.[^7]
> 
> You flow toward your goal, trackless as a wave,  
> And yet lie still, like the river’s shore.[^8]
> 
> Feminine beauty is lightning to your nature,  
> Yet strangely, your love remains aloof.[^9]
> 
> The law of your being is built on constant change;  
> Do you ever bow your head at a single threshold?[^10]
> 
> Among the beautiful, you are known as one who knows no loyalty;  
> O mercurial one, you are famous and infamous alike.[^11]
> 
> You have brought into this world the nature of quicksilver;  
> A marvel, this restlessness of yours; how strangely restless you are![^12]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 28 "Aashiq Harjaai" ([translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/28-aashiq-harjaai/translation/)).
[^2]-[^12]: Ibid., [p-59c9ed](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/28-aashiq-harjaai/translation/#p-59c9ed) through [p-bc0cec](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/28-aashiq-harjaai/translation/#p-bc0cec). (URLs constructed per standard corpus format from paragraph IDs in tool results; each links to the specific stanza.)
